name: Chocolate Chestnut Bourbon Torte w/ a Kentucky Bourbon Cream
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Chilled butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the cake: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour two nine-inch cake pans. Beat egg yolks well. Stir in 1 1/2 cups sugar and 4 tbsp bourbon, then blend in 2 1/2 cups chestnut puree. Fold in ground pecans. Whip egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff. Mix whites into batter. Divide mixture between cake pans and bake for 25 minutes. Cool on a rack, then remove from pans.
2. Make the filling: Beat 2 egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar. Add instant coffee and hot water. Beat chilled butter into mixture, then add 2 tbsp bourbon and remaining chestnut puree. Stir in chopped bitter chocolate. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
3. Melt bittersweet chocolate with cream over low heat. Stir in 4 tbsp bourbon, transfer to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
4. Assemble cake: Spread chilled chestnut filling on one layer, cover with other. Top with chocolate icing and chopped pecans. Chill before serving. Meanwhile, prepare bourbon chantilly: Whip cream to soft peaks, gradually beat in sugar, then fold in 2 tbsp bourbon.
